questions.question: In this context, choose the advanced pattern: She chose {a1} the issue directly.
After the preposition “to” in “objected to”, choose {a1}.
After the adjective, choose the natural infinitive: She was reluctant {a1} the mistake.
For purpose, choose {a1}: He called the office {a1} the time.
The earlier action needs a perfect infinitive: He seems {a1} the message earlier.
Use a passive infinitive: The contract needs {a1} today.
After the preposition, use a perfect gerund: She apologized for {a1} the speaker.
Use a passive gerund: He dislikes {a1} in public.
The object is explicit; choose the object + infinitive pattern: The manager encouraged us {a1}.
After “saw him”, choose the bare infinitive: I saw him {a1} the room.
A future task is meant; choose {a1}.
For a formal earlier claim, choose {a1}: The witness claimed {a1} the documents before.
